title: Slow-cooker lamb shanks
subtitle: Braised lamb shanks with red wine and tomatoes
excerpt: Lamb shanks browned and then braised in the slow cooker with red wine, tinned tomatoes, garlic, and thyme until the meat slides off the bone. One of the most rewarding things you can put in a slow cooker. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Braised lamb shanks are a classic of French and Italian cookery, where the shank is the standard cut for long braises (agnello brasato). The slow cooker reproduces the restaurant braise at home without attention. The red wine and tomato base is consistent across Italian, French, and Greek traditions. The technique is documented by Simon Hopkinson in Roast Chicken and Other Stories (1994) and Giorgio Locatelli in Made in Italy (2006).

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze in the sauce for up to three months.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make a day ahead. The sauce is better after an overnight rest.

content.text: Lamb shanks are the most dramatic cut in the slow cooker: a substantial bone-in piece that arrives looking architectural and leaves the table almost clean. The shank is all sinew and collagen, which after eight hours at low heat has converted entirely to gelatin, making the sauce unctuous and the meat almost frictionlessly tender. Brown every surface before they go in.

Season the shanks all over with {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt and {{black-pepper}} of pepper. Heat {{olive-oil}} of olive oil in a large frying pan over high heat. Brown the shanks on all sides for 8–10 minutes in total until well coloured. Transfer to the slow cooker. Reduce the heat to medium and fry the {{onion}} of chopped onion for 4 minutes. Add {{garlic}} of garlic and {{tomato-puree}} of tomato purée; stir for 1 minute. Pour in {{red-wine}} of red wine and bubble for 2 minutes to cook off the alcohol. Add {{tinned-tomatoes}} of tomatoes and {{stock-beef}} of stock. Pour over the shanks. Add the thyme. Cover and cook on LOW for 7–8 hours until the meat is falling from the bone. Remove the thyme. Taste the sauce and season. If the sauce is thin, pour it into a pan and reduce over high heat for 10 minutes. Serve with mashed potato or polenta.

items.symptom: Shanks won't fit in the slow cooker
items.cause: Most 6-litre slow cookers hold four shanks standing upright.
items.fix: Stand them bone-up in the pot. They will fit more easily and the sauce will cover the meat section at the base.

items.cause: Slow cooker accumulates liquid rather than reducing it.
items.fix: Pour the sauce into a wide saucepan and boil hard for 10 minutes until it coats the back of a spoon. The flavour concentrates at the same time.

items.cause: Overcooked — though this is rarely a problem for presentation, just move carefully.
items.fix: Reduce to 6 hours on LOW next time. The shank is ready when a skewer goes through the meat with no resistance.

content.text: Lamb shanks have the quality of a dish that takes effort to produce but almost none to serve. One shank per person, placed in a shallow bowl with sauce poured over and mash alongside, is a complete and impressive dinner. The slow cooker means that all the effort happens in the morning; by evening the work is done and the kitchen is clean.
